---
title: "S2S Lab 9"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
output: bookdown::gitbook
documentclass: book
link-citations: yes
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval=TRUE, comment=NA)
library(binom)
```

# Welcome!

Welcome to the 9th and 10th S2S labs (we'll use the same lab in both weeks). These labs on **confidence intervals** serve two purposes,

1. A reference for you to find all the formulas for calculating any confidence intervals relating to population means, variances and proportions, as covered in [Chapter 8](https://read.kortext.com/reader/pdf/92741/454) of *Probability and Statistics with R*. You will always have access to this lab in assessments, so make sure you are familiar with where to find these formulas.

2. Introduce some examples (in addition to those in the textbook) of calculating confidence intervals, for several different scenarios.
